chinese: adj. 個別的 (relating to a single person or a thing)
n. [C] 個人
example: The coach asked Mike to stay after class for some [individual] training.
Each [individual] in this company has the right to decide whether to go on the company trip or not.

chinese: vt.; vi. 錄影；錄音 (to make a copy of sounds or images)
vt. 記錄 (to keep or write down what has happened)
n. [C] 紀錄
example: Sandy [recorded] her favorite film, so she could watch it over and over again.
My father [recorded] in his diary that he met my mother on the first day of university.
Lisa is on a diet, so she keeps a [record] of what she eats every day.
notes: over and over again = repeatedly 重複地
keep a record of sth：保有某事的紀錄

chinese: vt. 重新安排；重新布置 (to change the order, position, or time of an event)
example: David had to [rearrange] his schedule after learning that the meeting had been moved to next Monday.

chinese: vt. 安排；布置
example: The students [arranged] the tables and chairs in rows after they finished using the classroom.

chinese: vt. 把握；抓住 (to make use of something quickly or hold someone)
example: The two sisters [seized] the opportunity to sing onstage in front of the talent show judges.
notes: seize the opportunity/moment：把握機會/時機
on/upon N：抓住（想法、機會、證據等）大做文章

chinese: adj. 密集的 (involving a lot of efforts or activities in a short time)
example: Irene took a two-week [intensive] English course before she went backpacking in the US.

chinese: adj. 典型的 (having the usual features or qualities of a particular group of things)
example: Stinky tofu, fried chicken, and bubble tea are [typical] Taiwanese snacks.
notes: a typical teenager：典型的青少年
be typical of...：不出所料的、一向如此的

chinese: n. [C][U] 例行公事 (things one regularly does)
example: Drinking a cup of coffee and reading the morning newspaper are two important parts of Julia's daily [routine].
notes: keep/stick to/follow a routine：養成生活作息

chinese: adj. 職業的、專業的 (relating to work that needs special training and skills)
example: It takes a lot of [professional] training to become a lawyer.

chinese: adj. 電子的 (relating to the use of electronic equipment, especially computers)
example: An [electronic] book is a type of digital book for people to read online.

chinese: vi.; vt. 進步，進展 (to develop or improve)
n. [C][U] 進步，進展
example: Over the past few years, the use of Augmented Reality (AR) in our daily lives has [advanced] greatly.
With [advances] in information technology, people can contact each other much more conveniently than before.

chinese: adj. 足夠的 (as much as is needed)
example: The ingredients on the table are [sufficient] for making two cakes.

chinese: n. [U] 設備；裝備 (the things needed for a particular activity or purpose)
example: Louis bought pieces of kitchen [equipment] after moving into his new apartment.

chinese: vt. 配備，裝備 (equip—equipped—equipped)
example: The newly-opened hotel, which is [equipped] with a swimming pool and a movie theater, is popular with tourists.

chinese: adj. 理想的 (the best or most suitable)
example: This park is an [ideal] place for outdoor activities and picnics.

chinese: vt.; vi. 播放，廣播 (broadcast—broadcast—broadcast) (to send out messages or programs on television or radio)
n. [C] (電視、廣播) 節目
example: A ball game will be [broadcast] live on television across the nation tonight.
My parents always watch the evening news [broadcast] after dinner every day.

chinese: adj. 全球的，全世界的 (involving or affecting the whole world)
example: Air pollution is a [global] environmental issue that everyone needs to work together to deal with.
notes: global brand：全球知名的品牌或人

chinese: adv. 海外地 (to or in a foreign country that is across the sea)
example: Emma works [overseas] and returns to her hometown twice a year.

chinese: vt. 犧牲 (to give up something valuable to get something more important)
n. [C][U] 犧牲
example: Michael [sacrificed] his sleeping time for an important meeting the next day.
Nick is willing to make any [sacrifice] to keep his child safe and happy.
notes: sacrifice A for B：為了 B 犧牲 A
at a great sacrifice/cost：做出巨大的犧牲

chinese: vt. 想像 (to create an image of something in one's mind)
example: The girl dreams about traveling to space, and she often [imagines] herself walking on the moon.
notes: imagine sb V-ing：想像某人做某事

chinese: n. [C][U] 想像力
example: The teacher asked me to use my [imagination] to guess the meaning of this painting.

chinese: n. [U] 電子競技
n. [C] 錦標賽
notes: race: 跟速度有關的比賽
match: 兩人或兩隊的比賽

chinese: n. [U] 團隊合作
adj. 全職的
n. 《爐石戰記》
n. [C] 壁爐底石
n. 《傳說對決》
n. [C] 競技場
n. [U] 英勇
n. [C][U] 友情
不得不 (to show one cannot avoid doing something)
example: Because of the heavy rain, Tony [had no choice but to] cancel his plan to play soccer outside.
notes: have no choice/option but to + V原
Because of = Due to = Owing to = On account of (因為...)

chinese: 補償 (to do something good for someone who is treated wrongly)
example: Ashley was late for the party, so she brought a lot of food to [make it up to] her friends.
notes: make up: 化妝、和好、彌補、捏造、組成

chinese: 代表 (to represent what something means, especially as a shorter form)
example: ASAP [stands for] "as soon as possible," which means at the earliest possible time.

chinese: 實現……的關鍵 (the most important thing to help one achieve something)
example: Regular exercise is the [key to] health and happiness.

chinese: 謀生 (to make enough money to live)
example: The street artist [makes a living] by painting pictures for tourists.

chinese: 其實，事實上 (used to add more details about what someone has just said)
example: I know our new classmate very well. [As a matter of fact], she is my neighbor.

chinese: 至少 (not less than)
example: It will take you [at least] an hour to see the doctor because there are many people waiting ahead of you.
